exercises.title: POWER RELAY
exercises.brief: A switch that flips between two states.
promptLines: Define a class **Toggle** with `constructor(on = false)`, `flip()` (inverts the state), and `isOn()` (returns the current boolean). A fresh `Toggle()` starts **off**.

tests.name: starts off by default
tests.code:
var t = new Toggle();
assertEqual(t.isOn(), false);

tests.name: flip turns it on
flips back and forth
honors a true start state
instances are independent

exercises.hint: Store this.on in the constructor; flip sets this.on = !this.on; isOn returns it.
exercises.lore: Pull the relay: light, dark, light. The grid breathes in two states.

exercises.title: TALLY CORE
exercises.brief: Keep a running total across every hit.
promptLines: Define a class **Accumulator** with `constructor()` (total starts at **0**), `add(n)` (adds `n` and **returns** the new running total), and `total()` (returns the current total).

tests.name: add returns the new total
total reflects all adds
starts at zero
tests.code:
var acc = new Accumulator();
assertEqual(acc.total(), 0);

tests.name: handles negative deltas
instances are independent

exercises.hint: Init this.sum = 0 in the constructor; add does this.sum += n; return this.sum.
exercises.lore: The core counts every credit that passes through. It never forgets a single unit.

exercises.title: CARGO HOLD
exercises.brief: Track distinct items in the hold.
promptLines: Define a class **Inventory** with `constructor()` (starts empty), `add(item)` (stores the item), `has(item)` (returns `true`/`false`), and `count()` (returns the number of **distinct** items — duplicates do not increase the count).

tests.name: has finds an added item
has is false for missing item
count is distinct
empty hold counts zero
tests.code:
var inv = new Inventory();
assert(inv.count() === 0 && inv.has('x') === false);

tests.name: instances are independent

exercises.hint: Back it with a Set: add -> this.items.add(item); count -> this.items.size; has -> this.items.has(item).
exercises.lore: The cargo manifest logs each unique crate once. Stack ten of the same and it still reads one line.

exercises.title: SIGNAL QUEUE
exercises.brief: First in, first out — the packet buffer.
promptLines: Define a class **Queue** with `enqueue(x)` (adds to the back), `dequeue()` (removes and returns the **front** item, or `null` when empty), and `size()` (number of items waiting). It is **FIFO**: the first item enqueued is the first dequeued.

tests.name: dequeues in FIFO order
size tracks pending items
empty dequeue returns null
tests.code:
var q = new Queue();
assertEqual(q.dequeue(), null);

tests.name: drains then refills
instances are independent

exercises.hint: Back it with an array: enqueue pushes; dequeue returns null when empty else shift(); size is items.length.
exercises.lore: Packets line up at the relay. The buffer spits them out in the order they arrived — no jumping the line.

exercises.title: CRED WALLET
promptLines: Define a class **Wallet** with `constructor(start = 0)`, `deposit(n)` (adds to the balance), `withdraw(n)` (returns `false` and changes **nothing** if `n` exceeds the balance, otherwise subtracts and returns `true`), and `balance()`.
solutionLines: withdraw(n) { if (n > this.bal) return false; this.bal -= n; return true; }

tests.name: deposit raises balance
withdraw succeeds when funded
tests.code:
var w = new Wallet(10);
assert(w.withdraw(4) === true && w.balance() === 6);

tests.name: overdraft is refused
tests.code:
var w = new Wallet(3);
assert(w.withdraw(9) === false && w.balance() === 3, 'no change on refusal');

tests.name: default start is zero
tests.code:
var w = new Wallet();
assert(w.balance() === 0 && w.withdraw(1) === false);

tests.name: exact balance withdraws fully
tests.code:
var w = new Wallet(5);
assert(w.withdraw(5) === true && w.balance() === 0);

tests.name: instances are independent

exercises.hint: Store this.bal in the constructor; in withdraw guard with if (n > this.bal) return false before subtracting.
exercises.lore: No overdraft in Night City. The wallet bounces you cold before it lets you go negative.
